summary: A crystal‑clear recording captures a high‑energy early‑80s Dead show highlighted by Shakedown Street, Deal, Help > Slipknot > Franklin's and an emotional Morning Dew.
review: The tape boasts excellent sound quality—clear, bright in the second set with only minor EQ/hiss quirks—and delivers a fun, loose performance that many fans rate among the best of ’83. Standout moments include Shakedown Street, Deal, a smoking Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s sequence and one of the most moving Morning Dew jams of the era.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews overwhelmingly praise both the musical performance and the recording clarity; only a few minor criticisms keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very clear, crisp sound with a noticeably brighter second‑set mix; reviewers note slight high‑end EQ variance and light hiss but overall superb fidelity.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans describe a strong first set bookended by Shakedown and Deal, followed by a bright second set featuring the classic HSF transition into an expansive Dew; overall pacing is energetic with occasional bright‑mix contrast.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Huge smile at the start (forgot Shakedown lyrics), flawless tone on Shakedown, smooth buttery solo on Lost Sailor, emotional lead on Morning Dew and reasserts his guitar voice after Brent’s momentary dominance.
band_member_comments.Phil: Smiling constantly, delivered “bombs” and a memorable St. of Circumstance solo; praised for crushing the Morning Dew and overall fun vibe.
band_member_comments.Bob: Little Red Rooster features what many call the best Bob Weir slide solo ever.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s simple piano sound shines, provides great licks throughout, occasionally overshadows Jerry during Dew but backs off later.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey and Billy wore bat masks for a theatrical drum solo, showcased talking‑drum textures, and delivered powerful, technically impressive playing.
